metadata.archetype_justification: Eureka maintains in-memory instance registry state with CRUD operations (register, renew, cancel, status update) exposed via REST endpoints. It owns mutable state (the registry) and exposes read/write operations on business entities (service instances).

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute — no IaC, no containers, no serverless. WAR deployment only.
top_gaps.impact: Cannot scale elastically, no container orchestration, high operational overhead.

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: Zero IaC — all infrastructure is undefined in repository.
top_gaps.impact: No reproducible infrastructure, no automated provisioning, no disaster recovery capability.

top_gaps.title: Secrets Management
top_gaps.gap: No application-level secrets management. CI/CD secrets not rotated.
top_gaps.impact: No rotation, no audit trail, no centralized secrets governance.

top_gaps.title: Deployment Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No deployment strategy — only library publishing to Maven Central.
top_gaps.impact: No safe rollout mechanism, no canary/blue-green capability, high-risk releases.

top_gaps.title: Programming Languages
top_gaps.gap: Java 8 with Jersey 1.x, AWS SDK v1, Servlet 2.5 — compound legacy.
top_gaps.impact: Blocked from modern AWS features, security patches, and cloud-native frameworks.

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Eureka has well-defined module boundaries but shared in-memory state. Full decomposition may not be architecturally appropriate for an in-memory registry. Containerize first, then evaluate whether AWS Cloud Map replaces the need for self-hosted Eureka.
phases: Selective extraction of eureka-client as standalone library
Evaluate migration to AWS Cloud Map (managed service discovery)

effort_factors.data_coupling: Shared in-memory registry state — Medium effort
effort_factors.cicd_maturity: Build/publish only — High effort for deployment pipeline
effort_factors.test_coverage: ~110 test files, some integration — Medium safety net

findings.title: No managed compute — WAR deployment with no containerization or serverless
findings.description: All compute is undefined in the repository. Application packaged as WAR for external servlet container. No ECS, EKS, Lambda, Fargate, or EC2 resource definitions exist.
findings.gap: No managed compute infrastructure. No containerization, no serverless, no IaC-defined compute resources.
findings.recommendation: Containerize: convert WAR to embedded server, create Dockerfile, deploy to EKS. Define EKS cluster and service resources in Terraform or CDK.

findings.title: Multi-step operations hardcoded with no orchestration service
findings.description: Peer replication batching, registry sync, and self-preservation are implemented as hardcoded application logic with custom batching frameworks.
findings.gap: No dedicated workflow orchestration service. Custom batching framework handles multi-step replication but with no managed orchestration or observability.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate Step Functions or EventBridge for peer replication workflow observability. The current in-process approach may be architecturally appropriate for latency-sensitive service discovery.

findings.title: No messaging infrastructure — peer replication via synchronous HTTP only
findings.description: No messaging infrastructure exists. All peer replication and client communication is synchronous HTTP. State changes propagated via synchronous HTTP batch requests.
findings.gap: State changes between Eureka server peers are propagated via synchronous HTTP. No async decoupling for cross-instance state propagation.
findings.recommendation: Evaluate EventBridge or SQS for peer state propagation. Alternatively, migrating to AWS Cloud Map eliminates the peer replication concern entirely.

findings.title: No network security infrastructure defined
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network policy definitions exist. No IaC-defined network topology.
findings.gap: Zero network security configuration. No VPC isolation, no private subnets, no security groups.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC with private subnets for Eureka servers, security groups restricting access to port 8080 from known CIDRs, and VPC endpoints for AWS service access.

findings.title: No API entry point — services exposed directly
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, CloudFront, or managed entry point defined. REST API exposed directly by servlet container.
findings.gap: Services exposed directly with no gateway, load balancer, throttling, or centralized authentication.
findings.recommendation: Deploy behind ALB (minimum) or API Gateway with throttling and authentication. For EKS, use AWS Load Balancer Controller.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists. AWS Auto Scaling SDK dependency is used for querying ASG membership, not for self-scaling.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling for the Eureka service. All capacity statically provisioned.
findings.recommendation: When deployed to EKS, configure HPA based on request rate or registry size.

findings.title: Zero Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm charts, or Kustomize files exist. All infrastructure must be manually created.
findings.gap: 0% IaC coverage. No infrastructure defined in code.
findings.recommendation: Add Terraform or CDK for EKS cluster, VPC, ALB, ECR, IAM, CloudWatch alarms, and Route 53 records.

findings.title: CI/CD limited to artifact publication — no deployment automation
findings.description: GitHub Actions workflows automate build and publish to Maven Central, but no deployment pipeline exists for production workloads.
findings.gap: Build automated but deployment entirely absent. No container build, no image push, no deployment step.
findings.recommendation: Extend GitHub Actions with Docker build, ECR push, Helm chart packaging, and EKS deployment. Add IaC pipeline for Terraform/CDK changes.

findings.title: Compound legacy — Java 8, Jersey 1.x, AWS SDK v1, Servlet 2.5
findings.description: Java 8 with Jersey 1.x (1.19.1), AWS SDK v1 (1.11.277), Servlet API 2.5. Compound regression across language version, framework, and SDK.
findings.gap: Language version AND framework AND SDK all regressed. Requires upgrades across all three axes.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Java 17+ with Spring Boot 3.x and AWS SDK v2. Sequence: Java 17 → Spring Boot 3.x → AWS SDK v2 → Micrometer.

findings.title: Monolith with identifiable modules but shared in-memory state
findings.description: 10 Gradle submodules with clear boundaries, but deployed as single WAR. Modules share in-memory registry state.
findings.gap: Single deployable unit. Shared in-memory state couples all server-side modules. Cannot independently scale or deploy modules.
findings.recommendation: Containerize as single service first. Evaluate AWS Cloud Map as replacement. Monolithic architecture may be correct for in-memory registry.

findings.title: All communication synchronous HTTP — no async patterns for state propagation
findings.description: All peer replication and client communication is synchronous HTTP. No async patterns exist for cross-service state changes.
findings.gap: Primarily synchronous with no async for peer state propagation where async would decouple peers.
findings.recommendation: Add EventBridge or SQS for peer state propagation. Keep client heartbeats synchronous. Alternatively, migrating to Cloud Map eliminates this.

findings.title: Background processing uses custom batching framework
findings.description: Peer replication batching uses homegrown async job processor (TaskExecutors, AcceptorExecutor). No managed service for background processing.
findings.gap: Background processing exists but uses untested custom framework rather than managed services.
findings.recommendation: Current internal batching likely adequate for service discovery. Ensure thread pool config is tunable via environment variables when containerizing.

findings.title: No versioning strategy — single /v2/ with no evolution plan
findings.description: REST API uses /v2/apps paths but this is a single version with no versioning strategy for evolution.
findings.gap: Single version with no versioning strategy. No backward compatibility guarantees or version negotiation.
findings.recommendation: Document V2 API contract. Add OpenAPI specification generation. Define versioning strategy and backward compatibility guarantees.

findings.title: Peer discovery uses static configuration — no dynamic service discovery for Eureka itself
findings.description: Peer Eureka servers discovered via static URL lists in properties files. DNS-based lookup supported but not default.
findings.gap: Peer discovery uses properties/environment variables rather than dynamic discovery. No service mesh or dynamic routing.
findings.recommendation: Use Kubernetes service DNS for peer discovery in EKS (headless Service). Or configure DNS-based discovery with Route 53.

findings.title: No CloudTrail or audit logging — only Log4j 1.x console output
findings.description: No CloudTrail configured. Application logging via Log4j 1.x (EOL) with console appender only. No structured logs, no retention.
findings.gap: No CloudTrail. No immutable audit logs. Log4j 1.x with console-only output.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudTrail for API-level auditing. Replace Log4j 1.x with Logback + JSON encoder. Ship to CloudWatch Logs with retention.

findings.title: No API authentication — all endpoints open
findings.description: ServerRequestAuthFilter logs identity headers but does NOT enforce authentication. All endpoints open and unauthenticated.
findings.gap: All API endpoints completely open. Any client can register/deregister without credentials.
findings.recommendation: Add mTLS for peer replication. Add API Gateway with OAuth2/JWT for external access. Consider Istio service mesh for zero-trust.

findings.title: No centralized identity integration
findings.description: No Cognito, Okta, SAML, OIDC, or centralized identity system. Application has no concept of user identity.
findings.gap: No centralized identity integration. No SSO, no federation, no external IdP.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with centralized IdP (Cognito for AWS-native). Use IRSA for EKS pod-level AWS credentials.

findings.title: No application-level secrets management — CI/CD secrets not rotated
findings.description: CI/CD uses GitHub Secrets (not plaintext). No application-level Secrets Manager or Vault. No rotation configured.
findings.gap: No rotation configured for CI/CD secrets. No Secrets Manager integration for application runtime.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS Secrets Manager for runtime secrets. Configure automated rotation for signing keys. Add IRSA for EKS pod credentials.

findings.title: No compute hardening or patching — vulnerable dependencies detected
findings.description: No patching strategy. No vulnerability scanning. Known-vulnerable dependencies: Log4j 1.x, XStream 1.4.19, Jetty 7.2.0.
findings.gap: No patching strategy. No vulnerability scanning. Known CVE-affected libraries in use.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot immediately. Use Bottlerocket/hardened images for EKS. Add ECR scanning. Upgrade Log4j 1.x to Logback.

findings.title: No security scanning in CI/CD pipeline
findings.description: No SAST, no dependency scanning, no container scanning. CI only runs gradlew build with no security validation.
findings.gap: Pipeline has no security validation. No Dependabot, no SAST, no container scanning.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot for dependency alerts. Add OWASP dependency-check Gradle plugin. Add Semgrep as CI step.

findings.title: No distributed tracing instrumented
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, Zipkin, or Jaeger. No trace ID propagation between clients and servers or between peers.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing. Debugging request flows requires manual log correlation.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry Java agent for auto-instrumentation. Configure X-Ray or OTEL collector for trace aggregation.

findings.title: Metrics exist internally (Servo) but not published externally
findings.description: Netflix Servo metrics (EurekaMonitors) track operations but are not published to any external monitoring system.
findings.gap: Comprehensive metrics defined in code but invisible outside JVM. No dashboards, no alerting, no trending.
findings.recommendation: Replace Servo with Micrometer. Publish to CloudWatch. Create operational dashboards.

findings.title: No alerting or anomaly detection configured
findings.description: No CloudWatch alarms, no PagerDuty/OpsGenie. Self-preservation is application-level only, not external alerting.
findings.gap: No external alerting. No anomaly detection. Only in-process self-preservation heuristic exists.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch alarms for registration rate drops, peer sync failures, error rates. Add anomaly detection on heartbeat rates.

findings.title: No deployment strategy — only artifact publication
findings.description: Repository publishes JARs/WARs to Maven Central. No production deployment pipeline, no canary, no blue/green.
findings.gap: No deployment strategy. No staged rollout. No deployment pipeline exists.
findings.recommendation: Implement rolling updates with health probes in EKS. For higher safety, add canary via Argo Rollouts.

findings.title: Minimal integration testing — one test among ~110
findings.description: Single integration test (EurekaClientServerRestIntegrationTest) among ~110 primarily unit test files. No peer replication or cluster tests.
findings.gap: One integration test. No peer replication tests, no multi-instance tests, no contract tests.
findings.recommendation: Add Testcontainers-based integration suite for multi-instance peer replication, client failover, and registry lifecycle.

findings.title: No incident response automation or runbooks
findings.description: No runbooks, no SSM Automation, no Lambda remediation, no self-healing beyond built-in self-preservation.
findings.gap: No runbooks. No automated incident response. Entirely ad hoc.
findings.recommendation: Create operational runbooks for peer sync failure, mass deregistration, self-preservation activation. Add pod restart automation.

findings.title: No observability ownership defined
findings.description: No CODEOWNERS for monitoring, no named alarm owners, no team dashboards, no SLO definitions with attribution.
findings.gap: No observability ownership. No assets exist to own.
findings.recommendation: Define ownership in CODEOWNERS when implementing observability. Assign alarm owners to on-call team.

findings.title: No resource tagging — no IaC-defined resources exist
findings.description: No resource tagging because no IaC-defined resources exist. Zero tags on zero resources.
findings.gap: No tagging governance. No resources to tag. Standards must be established when IaC is added.
findings.recommendation: Define mandatory tags from day one in IaC: Service, Environment, Team, CostCenter. Use default_tags in Terraform provider.

evaluations.reason: No persistent state to back up (has_persistent_data_store=false, has_at_rest_data_surface=false).
No database — no stored procedures possible.
No deployed workload for SLO definition (has_deployed_workload=false).

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=2 (primary trigger met) AND INF-Q1=1, APP-Q3=2 (supporting triggers met)

triggering_questions.note: Monolith with identifiable modules — below threshold of 3
No managed compute — below threshold of 3

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 AND no container definitions found in repository

triggering_questions.note: No managed compute, no containers — below threshold of 3

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No commercial database engines detected. System uses no database (in-memory registry only).
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected. DATA-Q4 Not Evaluated — no database exists.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No database deployed (has_persistent_data_store=false). INF-Q2 Not Evaluated.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: System has no persistent data store. INF-Q2 was Not Evaluated due to surface flag.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads exist. No streaming/ETL/analytics artifacts found.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No evidence of data processing workloads. No streaming, ETL, or analytics artifacts in repository.

triggering_questions.note: CI/CD for build only, no deployment — below threshold of 3
No deployment strategy — below threshold of 3
Minimal integration testing — below threshold of 3

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context is 'Netflix service-discovery server and client' — no AI-related signal terms found.
